behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the target files and methods (finish() in visitor/mod.rs, execute_visitor() in run/mod.rs) and all test commands.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the crash symptom without specifying Arc::try_unwrap or the is_watch guard as the fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_try_unwrap_pattern_exists requires 'Arc::try_unwrap' and test_watch_mode_guard_exists requires literal 'if !is_watch' pattern; alternative correct fixes (e.g., using Weak, restructuring ownership, or a different guard variable/method) would fail these literal-string checks.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most tests invoke cargo check/test/clippy/fmt as subprocesses; two are text-inspection but majority execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The cargo test and clippy invocations require compiled working code; they cannot be satisfied by stubs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Compile, clippy, watch tests, visitor tests, task filter tests, and fmt are all verified as specified in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings explaining each check.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names target files/methods at module level, which is acceptable scope guidance without revealing the exact lines or fix.
